body:
Metal.jl 1.10 implements the AbstractFFTs API for MtlArray, so wire it
into the FFT machinery like the CUDA and AMDGPU extensions:

* define plan_forward_transform/plan_backward_transform for MtlArray and
  drop the ConjugateGradientPoissonSolver override, so regular grids get
  FFTBasedPoissonSolver and stretched grids FourierTridiagonalPoissonSolver
* test the FFT
  and Fourier-tridiagonal solvers on Metal

---

This was mainly a pattern-matching task (we already support FFT-based solvers on other GPUs, it was a matter of copying the same pattern), so I gave the pattern-matching machine a go.  I reviewed the changes, and they made sense to me, and in a couple of cases where it wasn't clear to me the motivation for the changes, for example in `src/Solvers/discrete_transforms.jl` and `src/Solvers/index_permutations.jl`, I got it to add explanatory comments, which seemed reasonable.  Metal-related tests seem to pass locally.

labels.description: Where Oceananigans gets its powers from
labels.name: GPU 👾

title: Add support for FFT-based Poisson solvers on Metal GPUs

body: Let's see what's the situation nowadays.  I seem to remember Enzyme tests used to be the problem a few months ago, hopefully thins improved a little bit since then.
title: [CI] Run Enzyme/Reactant tests with Julia v1.11
